verdict.label: Worth checking
verdict.headline: Rey Cortiço Lunch
verdict.reason: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.

conversationalSummary.text: Rey Cortiço at Lunch looks like the best option, but it is still a check-first call. There is enough potential for a useful session, but the wind could spoil it. I'd check Surfline or MEO Beachcam before leaving.

best.windFit: W 15 km/h · cross-onshore · poor wind fit

photo.alt: Photo of Rey Cortiço surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Rey Cortiço looks like a small north coast window for Rare window, Medium confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 22 kJ, 9 s period, lully 0/100, and wind is the main risk.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 6-9s at mid tide can turn Belgas punchy and rippable, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot.
best.shortExplanation: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

ranked.explanation: Cerro looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0-0.3 m, 19 kJ, 9 s period, lully 0/100, and wind is not fully clear.  A sheltered beachbreak with gentle lefts and rights, somewhat similar to Cantinho but less consistent; this is best for manageable bodyboard waves when north-northwest swell reaches the bay with clean southerly wind; 6-9s at mid tide can make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia sharp and rippable; worth keeping on the radar because Surfline is showing it above Poor while most other local spots are Poor..  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; Cerro looks below your bodyboard fun size; Cerro may feel too soft without more push.
ranked.shortExplanation: Cerro is worth a quick check for after work (18:00 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: Cerro looks below your bodyboard fun size
Cerro may feel too soft without more push

ranked.explanation: Almagreira looks like a small window for Rare window, Medium confidence: 0-0.3 m, 21 kJ, 9 s period, lully 0/100, and wind is the main risk.  An exposed north-coast beach break with more raw swell than Baleal; this is best for small, clean north-coast days when protected spots look underpowered; 6-9s at mid tide can make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia sharp and rippable.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot; exposed north spot probably too small.
ranked.shortExplanation: Almagreira is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.

ranked.explanation: Meio da Baia looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0-0.3 m, 11 kJ, 9 s period, lully 0/100, and wind is the main risk.  An inconsistent Baía spot that can work well when north-northwest swell reaches the bay; this is best for bodyboard sessions when the bay is clean, there is enough period and size, and the forecast shows a defined wave; 6-9s at mid tide can make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia sharp and rippable; worth keeping on the radar because Surfline is showing it above Poor while most other local spots are Poor..  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; wind angle is poor for this spot; Meio da Baia looks below your bodyboard fun size; Meio da Baia may feel too soft without more push.
ranked.shortExplanation: Meio da Baia is worth a quick check for after work (18:00 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
